title: Iterating 🔄
description: Learn the best shortcuts for looping over your collections.

slides.title: Looping Over Lists 🏃‍♂️
The For-In Loop 🎯

quizData.question: What is the main benefit of using a `for-in` loop over a standard `for` loop for Lists?

options.text: It checks if the List has duplicates.
It handles finding the length and current index for you automatically.
It deletes the List when it finishes.

quizData.explanation: For-In instantly abstracts away the complex length/index management.

slides.title: .forEach() 🪄
slides.content:
## The Arrow Shortcut

Lists and Sets have a built-in method called `.forEach()`. It takes a function and runs it on every item.

Combine this with an arrow function for the ultimate one-liner:

`weapons.forEach((w) => print(w));`

*Mind blown!* 🤯

quizData.question:
Fill in the blank to print every item in a single line:
players.forEach((p) __ print(p));
quizData.explanation: The arrow function => makes .forEach() incredibly clean.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: To loop over a Map, you can use `.forEach()` which provides BOTH the Key and the Value!
